sourceMovie.title: Going Places
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Going Places (1974) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Bertrand Blier's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Chasing Amy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Going Places clicked for you, Chasing Amy (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Going Places?
faq.answer: Try Chasing Amy, Wicked Little Letters, Slacker—each shares DNA with Going Places while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Going Places?
faq.answer: Going Places (1974) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Going Places worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
